headline: 3 Reasons We’re Fans of Alphabet (GOOGL)
summary: Alphabet has been on fire lately. In the past six months alone, the company’s stock price has rocketed 46%, setting a new 52-week high of $403.66 per share. This was partly due to its solid quarterly results, and the run-up might have investors contemplating their next move.

headline: Berkshire Hathaway Portfolio Under Greg Abel Shifts Toward Alphabet And Delta
summary: Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE:BRK.A) recorded its first quarter of portfolio decisions under new CEO Greg Abel. The company exited positions in Amazon, Visa, Mastercard, and UnitedHealth while sharply reducing the overall number of holdings. Berkshire returned to airline stocks with a new stake in Delta Air Lines and added exposure to Macy's, while reallocating more capital to Alphabet. Berkshire Hathaway enters this new phase with NYSE:BRK.A recently trading around $723,821.0 per share. Over...

headline: Alphabet’s $60b Bond Spree Reshapes AI Spending And Investor Tradeoffs
summary: Alphabet (NasdaqGS:GOOGL) has raised nearly US$60b in global bonds over the past four months to fund AI, cloud, and data center investments. In the past week, the company completed the largest-ever yen bond issuance by a foreign issuer, adding to earlier euro and Canadian dollar deals. The multi-currency bonds extend across long maturities, creating multi-decade refinancing considerations and higher leverage for Alphabet. Alphabet sits at the center of large scale AI and cloud spending,...

headline: Why Wayfair (W) Stock Is Down Today
summary: Shares of online home goods retailer Wayfair (NYSE:W) fell 2.7% in the afternoon session after the April PPI report lifted the 10-year Treasury yield to a 10-month high of 4.49%, eliminating 2026 rate-cut expectations and raising the discount rate for long-duration growth valuations.

summary: The data underscores an uncomfortable reality.

headline: Why AI layoffs aren't giving stocks the boost companies wanted
summary: AI-driven layoffs have become a defining theme across the tech industry over the past year, with some sources estimating more than 112,000 U.S. jobs being lost to AI since the start of 2025. While major companies like HP and Amazon have often framed these cuts as a way to boost efficiency and shift focus toward new technologies, investors themselves don't appear convinced that these layoffs will improve companies' bottom lines in the long run.

headline: Is It Too Late To Consider Alphabet (GOOGL) After Its 140% One-Year Surge?
summary: If you are wondering whether Alphabet's current share price still makes sense for new capital, it starts with understanding what the market might be pricing in versus what the underlying business could be worth. The stock last closed at US$396.78, with returns of 16.1% over the past 30 days and 25.9% year to date, while the past year shows a 139.6% gain and the past 5 years show an increase that is roughly 2.5x the starting level, even with a slight 1% pullback over the past week.
